evidence_quotes.quote: As a lifelong digital gamer, I have amassed a collection of games over decades, cherished mementos shared with my family.
Protecting our right to preserve and enjoy these digital legacies is crucial.
We are the owners of these copies, and it is vital that we can continue to enjoy them.

paragraphs.text: Greetings, I am writing to express my strong support for the "Stop Killing Games" Act and the Digital Fairness Act. As a lifelong digital gamer, I have amassed a collection of games over decades, cherished mementos shared with my family. These games represent invaluable moments of connection and shared joy, as significant to me as a family photo or heirloom. Protecting our right to preserve and enjoy these digital legacies is crucial. Years ago, I restored a classic family car. Finding original parts was challenging, yet we maintained the right to keep it runninga symbol of family history and shared memories. We revived those memories, rekindling smiles and shared experiences. No one argued that the car should be destroyed simply because it's no longer a current model. My father's hard-earned 1970s car, passed down to me, holds immense sentimental value. I want to pass on my gaming legacy to future generations, sharing those same joyful memories. These acts are essential to ensuring this right is protected. Many of my games were purchased with money earned through hard work as a child. The right to continue using these games, regardless of whether they are supported by the studios, should be protected. Consider allowing them to become freeware or open-source, enabling fans like myself to preserve them for future generations. Just as we restore classic cars, gamers can restore and preserve digital games fueled by nostalgia and dedication. We are the owners of these copies, and it is vital that we can continue to enjoy them. I strongly support these acts. They are essential for preserving our gaming heritage and sharing these cherished memories with those who follow.
